items.title: Global Capitalism Bets It All on AI Future Alarming Voters
items.content: Days after filing confidentially to go public, Anthropic, the $965 billion artificial intelligence juggernaut that’s one of the fastest-growing startups of all time, dropped another bombshell.
items.contentSnippet: Days after filing confidentially to go public, Anthropic, the $965 billion artificial intelligence juggernaut that’s one of the fastest-growing startups of all time, dropped another bombshell.
items.creator: Shirin Ghaffary, Enda Curran

items.title: Why the US Is Investing in Quantum Computing
items.content: The Trump administration is making another move into venture-style investing, taking equity stakes in nine quantum computing companies. IBM, the largest recipient of the investment, thinks that quantum systems could eventually solve problems involving drug discovery, financial markets, cybersecurity, logistics, and climate science that remain beyond the reach of classical computers. But while companies such as IBM and IonQ claim major breakthroughs are near, former IBM CEO Sam Palmisano questions the government’s role in choosing specific corporate winners and warns that commercial adoption may still take years to realize.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: OpenAI Probed by Coalition of State Attorneys General
items.content: OpenAI is under investigation by a coalition of state attorneys general who requested information from the artificial intelligence company on a wide range of topics.

items.title: Wall Street Week | SpaceX Goes Public, Google’s AI Bet, World Cup Price Backlash
items.content: This week, the arrival of mega-IPOs is testing capital markets as investor appetite grows larger than supply. The federal government is making a direct investment in private technology companies, betting on quantum computing. Plus, Google is making its most radical change to search in twenty years by incorporating AI. Later, the most lucrative World Cup in history is testing how much fans are willing and able to pay for football.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX Jumps in First Day Following Record $75B IPO | The Close 6/12/2026
items.content: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are The IPO Initiative Director Jay Ritter, Impulse Space Founder & CEO Tom Mueller, Pitchbook Senior Research Analyst Franco Granda, Class V Group Founder Lise Buyer, Kayne Anderson Rudnick Portfolio Manager & Senior Research Analyst Julie Biel, Neuberger Senior Portfolio Manager Dan Hanson, Space Foundation CEO Heather Pringle, NYC Comptroller Mark Levine, Former Us Senator and 2X NBA Champion Bill Bradley, & Big3 Co-Founder & Rapper Ice Cube. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Paramount’s Deal for Warner Bros. Is Cleared by US DOJ
items.content: The US Justice Department has closed an antitrust probe into Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion purchase of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., saying the deal “is not likely” to hurt consumers or competition in the film and television industry.
items.contentSnippet: The US Justice Department has closed an antitrust probe into Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion purchase of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., saying the deal “is not likely” to hurt consumers or competition in the film and television industry.
items.creator: Leah Nylen, Josh Sisco

items.title: Class V Group Founder Analyzes Unique Features of SpaceX's Record $75B IPO
items.content: Lise Buyer, Founder at Class V Group and one of the architects of Google's IPO, provided insights on SpaceX's recent record-breaking $75 billion IPO. She highlighted the unusual aspects of the offering, including its fixed pricing rather than a price range, and the staggered lockup release structure. She speaks to Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Lise Buyer, Founder at Class V Group and one of the architects of Google's IPO, provided insights on SpaceX's recent record-breaking $75 billion IPO. She speaks to Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Tom Mueller on SpaceX’s Rise and Space Economy
items.content: Tom Mueller, Founder and CEO of Impulse Space and first employee at SpaceX, discussed SpaceX’s evolution from a risky startup in 2002 to a $2 trillion public company. He described early skepticism, repeated test failures, and key milestones such as reaching orbit, servicing the ISS, landing reusable rockets, and enabling global internet. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Tom Mueller, Founder and CEO of Impulse Space and first employee at SpaceX, discussed SpaceX’s evolution from a risky startup in 2002 to a $2 trillion public company. items.title: SpaceX IPO Leaves Billions on the Table Amid High Valuation Concerns
items.content: Jay Ritter, director of The IPO Initiative and Emeritus Professor at the University of Florida, discussed the unprecedented $75 billion SpaceX IPO, highlighting its fixed $135 offer price set before the roadshow—a highly unusual approach. This pricing strategy resulted in a first-day stock price jump that left approximately $20 billion in first-day profits on the table, more than double the previous U.S. IPO record held by Alibaba. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX's Wall Street Debut Breaks Records
items.content: On today’s Big Take podcast, David Gura and Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow break down the over $1.7 trillion SpaceX IPO and what it means for Musk, the space economy and the financial markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX Shares Close 19% Higher After Historic $75 Billion IPO
items.content: SpaceX’s first day on the stock market transformed the startup into one of the world’s most-valuable public companies, handed buyers of the IPO a 19% return and turned its founder Elon Musk into the world’s first trillionaire.

items.title: SpaceX and Tesla Will Merge, Merz Says
items.content: Tesla investor Alexandra Merz says Tesla and SpaceX will merge. She speaks on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Tesla investor Alexandra Merz says Tesla and SpaceX will merge. She speaks on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX Rented Out Computing After Own Teams Had Trouble Using It
items.content: SpaceX decided to rent out the full capacity of its Colossus 1 data center in Memphis to Anthropic PBC after encountering technical challenges using the facility to develop and run its Grok artificial intelligence models, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: SpaceX Surges 11% In Public Debut | Open Interest 6/12/2026
items.content: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." SpaceX explodes out of the gate, opening 11% above its IPO price after raising $75 billion and instantly making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. Max Chafkin and Ed Ludlow break down Musk's growing grip on SpaceX, Tesla merger speculation, and what's next for investors. Plus, Interstellar Lab's Barbara Belvisi on how space farming technology could help humans live on the Moon and Mars. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX Shares Surge After IPO Makes Musk a Trillionaire
items.content: After months of waiting, SpaceX is now a public company. The stock climbed 31% above its offering price at at one point on the Nasdaq. Bloomberg's Yahaira Anand was there for opening bell and has more. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: After months of waiting, SpaceX is now a public company. The stock climbed 31% above its offering price at at one point on the Nasdaq. Bloomberg's Yahaira Anand was there for opening bell and has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX Makes History With Biggest-Ever IPO | Bloomberg Tech 6/12/2026
items.content: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow break down SpaceX's big debut in the public markets as shares start trading on the Nasdaq. They are joined by 137 Ventures Partner Christian Garrett, Pegasus Tech Ventures Founder Anis Uzzaman, Laffer Tengler Investments CEO & CIO Nancy Tengler, and Tesla retail investor Alexandra Merz to discuss what it means for investors, the space economy, and the AI IPO pipeline. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Elon Musk Is the World's First Trillionaire
items.content: It's official: The world has its first trillionaire. At $150 a share, Elon Musk’s net worth is roughly $1.05 trillion. SpaceX began trading on the Nasdaq Friday morning. Caroline Hyde, Ed Ludlow and Alexandra Merz, a Tesla investor, discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX Begins Trading on the Nasdaq After Record IPO
items.content: SpaceX is now trading. The stock sold for $150 in its opening trade on Nasdaq at 11:46 a.m. in New York, 11% above its $135 offering price, and climbed as high as $168.75 in the first few minutes. Ed Ludlow and Caroline Hyde report. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.content: Retail investors in the UK bought about $364 million worth of SpaceX shares, a small slice of the world’s largest-ever initial public offering.

items.title: SpaceX Isn’t in the S&P 500. That's Good.
items.content: The S&P 500 shouldn’t make exceptions for big tech companies like SpaceX, Anthropic or OpenAI. Keeping its criteria for admission is more important, says Bloomberg Opinion columnist Nir Kaissar. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Founders Fund, Andreessen Poised for Record Returns From SpaceX IPO
items.content: A small number of firms are set to net tens of billions of dollars in returns from SpaceX’s initial public offering at a nearly $1.8 trillion valuation, marking one of the most significant windfalls in venture capital history.

items.title: Elon Musk Talks SpaceX, Future, and History on IPO Day
items.content: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk kicked off a dual-listed Nasdaq bell ringing ceremony in both New York and Texas on Friday, with a livestream event where he addressed SpaceX employees. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Elon Musk Rings Nasdaq Opening Bell Before SpaceX Begins Trading
items.content: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk kicked off a dual-listed Nasdaq bell ringing ceremony in both New York and Texas on Friday morning before his company began trading. The rocket company made history with a $75 billion IPO. (Source: Bloomberg)
